Java developer Full-time
Tech Mahindra Date - Date
•Team Size: 5
•Environment: Java, Mysql, Shell script
•Webserver: Jenkins
•OR-Formatter is an application which takes the input file as Geneva Modified File GMF from Geneva server and
reads the data to generate Bill backup and Bill Invoices for Client customers of BT. These invoices would be sent
across to all the clients after billing.
•Played the role of Java developer involved in applying change request to current modules.
•Worked on requirement analysis and development for the user stories and also preformed dev unit testing for the
user stories.
•Handled E2E issues and fixing bugs.
•Prepare required program level and user-level documentation.
Java developer Full-time
Tech Mahindra Date - Date
•Team Size: 5
•Environment: Java EJB, Mysql, Shell script.
•Webserver: Weblogic12c Server, Jenkins.
•STAA is an encapsulation middleware layer, acting as an interface between client’s operational support systems and
Legacy systems. It transforms the legacy data into various formats such as TLV . Input is in form xml/http to STAA
where validations are done and the response is sent to the back end systems where back end systems are CSS and
COSMOSS Databases. The business methods deployed as EJBs on WebLogic Server shared across various market
facing units of client.
•Played the role of Java developer involved in creating services using Java/J2EE.
•Worked on requirement analysis and development for the user stories and also preformed dev unit testing for the
user stories.
•Handled E2E issues and fixing bugs.
•Worked in Migration from java 1.6 to java 1.8.
Skills
Computer Languages: Java/J2EE - Swift - HTML - Shell script
Databases: MySQL
Tools: SVN - Jenkins - Hudson - Weblogic12c
Software: Android Studio - Eclipse - Oracle - Xcode
Operating Systems: Win 10 - Mac (High Sierra)

Sara Hassan is a Java Developer with contact information including email (sara.hassan@email.com), phone number (+20 123 456 7890), and links to her GitHub (github.com/sara-hassan) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/sara-hassan) profiles, as well as her personal website (sarahassan.com).

Sara Hassan received a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology in June 2016, located in Goregaon, India. She also attended Thakur Polytechnic in May 2013 in Mumbai, India, and St. John’s Universal School in May 2010, also in Mumbai, India.

Sara Hassan worked as a Java developer at Tech Mahindra, where she was part of a team of 5, utilizing technologies like Java, MySQL, and Shell script, with Jenkins as the webserver. She worked on the OR-Formatter application, which generates billing invoices for BT clients, and was involved in change requests, requirement analysis, development, unit testing, bug fixing, and documentation.

Sara Hassan also worked as a Java developer at Tech Mahindra, utilizing Java EJB, MySQL, and Shell script, with Weblogic12c Server and Jenkins as webservers. She worked on STAA, a middleware layer that transforms legacy data into formats like TLV, and was involved in creating services, requirement analysis, development, unit testing, bug fixing, and migration from Java 1.6 to Java 1.8.

Sara Hassan possesses skills in various computer languages including Java/J2EE, Swift, HTML, and Shell script, databases like MySQL, tools such as SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, and Weblogic12c, software including Android Studio, Eclipse, Oracle, and Xcode, and operating systems like Win 10 and Mac (High Sierra).

Tech Mahindra is a technology services powerhouse, a bustling hub where skilled Java developers like Sara Hassan build and maintain critical systems for major clients. It’s a place of constant evolution, evidenced by projects like the OR-Formatter – a vital application generating billing invoices for BT, demanding meticulous attention to change requests, rigorous testing, and detailed documentation. But Tech Mahindra isn’t solely focused on maintaining existing infrastructure; it’s also a driver of modernization. Sara’s work on STAA, a middleware layer transforming legacy data into contemporary formats like TLV, showcases this commitment. The company deftly navigates complex technological landscapes, seamlessly integrating Java EJB, MySQL databases, and Shell scripting, all orchestrated through tools like Jenkins and Weblogic12c Server. A pragmatic approach to upgrades is also key – exemplified by the migration from Java 1.6 to the more current 1.8, ensuring systems remain robust and efficient. Tech Mahindra thrives on a foundation of core technologies – Java, MySQL, and Shell Script – consistently leveraged across diverse projects, and underpinned by automation through Jenkins. It’s a company that doesn’t just *use* technology, but actively shapes it to meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Sara Hassan is a resourceful Java Developer with a solid foundation built upon a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology. Her journey began with early education at St. John’s Universal School and Thakur Polytechnic in Mumbai, shaping a pragmatic and detail-oriented approach to problem-solving. 

Currently, she’s a proficient coder, comfortable navigating the complexities of Java/J2EE, Swift, HTML, and Shell scripting. She’s equally adept at database management with MySQL and utilizes a broad toolkit including SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, and Weblogic12c. Her development environment spans both Win 10 and Mac (High Sierra), and she’s proficient with IDEs like Android Studio, Eclipse, Oracle, and Xcode.

Her experience at Tech Mahindra has been focused on building and maintaining critical applications. She played a key role in the OR-Formatter project, generating billing invoices for BT clients – a task demanding precision and a full understanding of the development lifecycle, from requirement analysis and testing to bug fixing and documentation.  She also contributed significantly to STAA, a middleware layer responsible for transforming legacy data, even spearheading a successful migration from Java 1.6 to the more modern Java 1.8.  She’s comfortable handling end-to-end issues and thrives in collaborative team environments, having worked within a team of five. 

You can find her work and connect with her through GitHub (github.com/sara-hassan), LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/sara-hassan), or her personal website (sarahassan.com). She can also be reached via email at sara.hassan@email.com or by phone at +20 123 456 7890.

Shell Script is a versatile, often unassuming, workhorse within the digital landscape. Not a glamorous language like Java or Swift, it’s the pragmatic tool favored for automating tasks and gluing together complex systems. At Tech Mahindra, it’s a foundational element, quietly powering critical processes – from generating billing invoices for BT clients via the OR-Formatter application, to transforming legacy data within the STAA middleware layer. 

It’s a skill honed by developers like Sara Hassan, who wields it alongside more prominent languages, demonstrating its importance in a full-stack environment. While not the centerpiece of her projects, Shell Script consistently appears as a vital supporting player, handling the practicalities of deployment, data manipulation, and system maintenance. 

Think of it as the reliable stagehand of the software world – not seeking the spotlight, but ensuring everything runs smoothly behind the scenes. It thrives in environments alongside tools like Jenkins and databases like MySQL, proving its adaptability and enduring relevance in both legacy and modern systems. It’s a language of efficiency, favored for its direct access to operating system functionalities and its ability to streamline repetitive processes, a testament to its enduring utility in the hands of a skilled professional.

Sara Hassan is a meticulous and versatile Java Developer, a builder of digital bridges between complex systems and clear results. She thrives in collaborative environments, having consistently contributed as a key member of teams – notably within Tech Mahindra. Her skillset is grounded in a strong command of Java, complemented by proficiency in MySQL and Shell scripting. She’s adept at navigating the full software development lifecycle, from dissecting requirements and crafting solutions to rigorous unit testing and diligent bug fixing, always ensuring thorough documentation accompanies her work. 

Sara’s experience centers around two significant projects. First, the OR-Formatter application, a critical component for generating billing invoices for BT clients, where she expertly managed change requests and honed her development skills. Secondly, she played a vital role in the STAA project, a middleware layer responsible for transforming legacy data into modern formats like TLV. This involved not only creating new services but also a successful migration from Java 1.6 to the more current Java 1.8, demonstrating her adaptability and commitment to staying current with industry standards. 

She’s comfortable deploying solutions utilizing webservers like Jenkins and Weblogic12c, and possesses a practical understanding of continuous integration pipelines. Beyond her technical capabilities, Sara maintains an active online presence, showcasing her work and connecting with the developer community through GitHub (github.com/sara-hassan) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/sara-hassan), and a personal website (sarahassan.com). You can reach her directly at sara.hassan@email.com or +20 123 456 7890.

MySQL is a robust and widely-utilized relational database management system, a quiet workhorse powering applications across diverse landscapes. It’s a foundational element within Tech Mahindra, consistently employed by developers like Sara Hassan in critical projects. Not merely a tool, it’s a core competency for professionals like Sara, who leverages its capabilities alongside Java and Shell scripting to build and maintain essential systems. 

In the OR-Formatter application, MySQL diligently stores and retrieves data crucial for generating billing invoices for BT clients – a silent partner in financial transactions. Similarly, within the STAA middleware layer, it serves as the repository for transformed legacy data, expertly handling formats like TLV. It’s a dependable presence, supporting both established systems and migrations to newer Java versions (from 1.6 to 1.8, as seen in Sara’s work). 

MySQL isn’t flashy, but it’s undeniably essential – a reliable database underpinning the functionality of applications built with tools like Jenkins and Weblogic, and wielded by skilled developers ensuring data integrity and smooth operation. It’s a cornerstone of Sara Hassan’s skillset, a testament to its enduring relevance in the modern software development world.

Jenkins is a quietly essential tool, a digital workhorse humming beneath the surface of complex software development. It’s a webserver, often found alongside platforms like Weblogic12c, facilitating the automated building, testing, and deployment of applications. At Tech Mahindra, it’s a cornerstone of the development process, utilized by Java developers like Sara Hassan across multiple projects. 

It’s the engine powering the OR-Formatter application – the system that meticulously generates billing invoices for BT clients – and a key component in the STAA middleware layer, responsible for transforming legacy data into modern formats. Jenkins isn’t about flashy features; it’s about reliability and efficiency. It handles the repetitive, crucial tasks of change requests, unit testing, and bug fixing, freeing developers to focus on innovation. 

Sara Hassan’s skillset demonstrates its broad applicability – comfortable alongside tools like Hudson and SVN, Jenkins is a familiar presence for those fluent in Java, MySQL, and Shell scripting. It’s a testament to its enduring value that it remains a relevant skill even as technologies evolve, supporting migrations from older Java versions to the latest standards. Jenkins doesn’t *create* the software, but it ensures it’s built, tested, and delivered smoothly, a silent partner in the creation of functional, reliable systems.
